title: Phunware acquires stake in political tech firm MyCanvass
text: Phunware acquires stake in political tech firm MyCanvass. Key Details: Phunware, Inc. (NASDAQ: PHUN) has agreed to acquire a controlling interest in MyCanvass, LLC, a political technology firm specializing in voter and advocacy engagement tools. The deal is valued at $1.02 million and will be funded through cash and stock. MyCanvass is currently owned by Campaign Nucleus, a platform founded by Brad Parscale, who served as Digital Director for Donald Trump's 2016 election campaign and Campaign Manager for Trump's 2020 candidacy. The company provides services for Trump's 2024 campaign. Strategic Goals: The partnership aims to develop AI-powered tools for managing canvassing operations and enhancing voter mobilization. Phunware CEO Stephen Chen expressed enthusiasm about combining the platforms, emphasizing their shared mission to leverage technology while maintaining transparency and accountability in campaigns. Market Context: According to InvestingPro data, Phunware demonstrated strong revenue growth of 232.95% over the preceding twelve months, positioning the company well for expansion into the political technology sector.
